question.content: For the coupled cylinder-block system on the incline, compute the acceleration magnitude a from the dynamic equations. Output exactly one decimal number with 2 digits after the decimal point, plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no percent sign, no fraction, no equation form, no sentence, and no extra text. Answer format: a decimal number rounded to two decimal places; write only the numerical value itself, with no units, explanatory text, or equals sign.

step_1.content: Virtual-work / equivalent-mass method: use the common rope displacement s along the incline as the single generalized coordinate, then obtain acceleration from generalized driving work divided by generalized inertia.
step_1.key_point: This route avoids writing separate translation and rotation equations for each body.

step_3.content: Because the connector is inextensible, the block and the cylinder center share the same speed along the incline. The whole motion can therefore be described by one coordinate s and one acceleration a=s_ddot.
step_3.key_point: The coupled system is reduced to a single admissible virtual displacement.

step_5.content: Rolling without slip gives the cylinder spin rate from the same coordinate s, so its rotational kinetic term can be rewritten as an added translational inertia I/r^2. Thus the generalized mass becomes m_2+m_1+I/r^2.
step_5.key_point: Rotation is absorbed into one equivalent mass contribution rather than handled by a separate torque equation.

step_7.content: Project all external actions onto the same positive s direction. Gravity of both masses contributes +(m_1+m_2)g sin α, while the rough contact on m_2 contributes the opposing term μ m_2 g cos α.
step_7.key_point: The generalized driving force is obtained directly from virtual work along the single coordinate.

step_8.content: The whole system now behaves like one generalized coordinate with one generalized driving term and one equivalent inertia. Substitute I=m_1 r^2/2, then evaluate the resulting acceleration numerically.
step_8.key_point: After replacing I/r^2 by m_1/2, the denominator reduces to 1.5m_1+m_2 before the final substitution.
